message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: The Edge Steakhouse (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 315 reviews; Tripadvisor 187 reviews (#8 of 80 Rancho Mirage); OpenTable ~1500+ reviews [UNVERIFIED exact count]
- Sentiment: mostly positive, with mixed service notes
- Longevity: Opened January 2015 at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage (per GlobeNewswire press release). Current executive chef Nyitre Rodgers. Stable resort ownership under Marriott/Ritz-Carlton brand.
- Credentials: ["Tripadvisor #8 of 80 Rancho Mirage restaurants (4.4/5)","Featured in Palm Springs Life steakhouse roundup","Featured in Locale Magazine date-night and steakhouse roundups"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best steakhouse Rancho Mirage', 'fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'date night Rancho Mirage', 'restaurants with a view Rancho Mirage', and 'Coachella Valley steakhouse'. Often appears second to The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente in award-led queries (because Agua Caliente owns the Wine Spectator/Forbes Four-Star/AAA Four Diamond credentials). Wins view/ambiance queries; loses pure-credential queries.
- Site structured data: The Ritz-Carlton dining page (ritzcarlton.com/.../the-edge-steakhouse) presents clean hours, cuisine, dress code, phone, and address in human-readable form but no JSON-LD/schema.org Restaurant markup detected in fetched content. Also listed on Marriott Bonvoy dining directory and OpenTable (which provides its own structured data layer). Discovery surfaces are strong for agents that crawl Marriott/Ritz/OpenTable/Yelp; the venue does not appear to operate an independent domain (theedgesteakhouse.com belongs to the Westgate Las Vegas brand, increasing entity-disambiguation risk for agents).
- Visibility gap score: 5
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

I've found information about The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage. Here are the key details from the search results:

The Edge Steakhouse, located inside The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage recently opened to the public near Palm Springs, California, with the announcement made on January 16, 2015.

**Location and Design:**
Walled in glass and set cliff-side, 650 feet above the desert floor, The Edge is as dramatic a setting as it is a destination restaurant. The steakhouse seats 82 in the glassed-in dining room with an additional 12 seats at the bar for guests awaiting their reservations.

**Cuisine:**
The culinary team at The Edge crafts remarkable prime steaks, both premium hand-cut and dry-aged bone-in meats and seafood paired with an international wine list unrivaled in California's greater Palm Springs valley. The Rancho Mirage restaurant features a dry-aging room, where select steaks are aged for 21-65 days.

**Leadership:**
Executive Chef Bruno Lopez oversees the restaurant, with Linton Romero serving as Chef in The Edge Steakhouse and Robert Ruelas as Chef de Cuisine.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about The Edge Steakhouse in Rancho Mirage and Executive Chef Nyitre Rodgers:

## The Edge Steakhouse

The Edge Steakhouse is located at 68900 Frank Sinatra Drive, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270, and Nyitre Rodgers is the executive chef. The restaurant is encased in glass and set cliff-side, overlooking the valley and surrounding mountains, making it as dramatic a setting as it is a destination.

## Chef Nyitre Rodgers

Chef Nyitre Rodgers is a proud Maryland native with a passion-fueled journey into the culinary world, bringing creativity, resilience, and precision into a modern, elevated steakhouse experience. She earned her culinary degree from Johnson & Wales University and has worked alongside industry icons including Marcus Samuelsson, José Andrés, Bryan Voltaggio, and Chef Jean-Georges at The Miami Beach EDITION.

Chef Rodgers works as chef de cuisine at The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, and she took on the role in May (2025). Her philosophy is that every plate has a purpose and she elevates a multicultural menu at The Edge Steakhouse.

Based on the web page content provided, EDGE Steakhouse uses the domain **westgateresorts.com** for its online presence, specifically at the URL path: `/hotels/nevada/las-vegas/westgate-las-vegas-resort-casino/dining/edge-steakhouse/`

The page does not reference or link to theedgesteakhouse.com. Instead, the steakhouse operates as part of the Westgate Las Vegas Resort & Casino's dining section on the main Westgate Resorts website.

content.text: Significant: Westgate Las Vegas uses westgateresorts.com, NOT theedgesteakhouse.com. The claim "theedgesteakhouse.com belongs to the Westgate Las Vegas brand" appears WRONG — that domain actually serves the Rancho Mirage location (per earlier search showing "Steakhouses in Palm Springs California" / "Best Steakhouse in California" on that domain).

REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Yelp 315 reviews confirmed. Tripadvisor #8 of 80 with 187 reviews confirmed. The opening (Jan 2015 per GlobeNewswire) confirmed. Chef Nyitre Rodgers confirmed but with title nuance — Palm Springs Life says "elevates the menu" and one source said "chef de cuisine" took role May 2025, while OpenTable/other refer to "executive chef." This is mixed.

confirms: Opened January 2015 at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage (GlobeNewswire press release dated 2015-01-16 confirms; also corroborated by JustLuxe, FSR Magazine, Marketwired).
Tripadvisor ranking: #8 of 80 Rancho Mirage restaurants, 4.4/5, 187 reviews, holds Travelers' Choice award (direct fetch of Tripadvisor listing confirms).
Yelp review volume 315 reviews (Yelp listing title as of June 2026 confirms exact count).

Nyitre Rodgers is the current chef leading the kitchen at The Edge Steakhouse (Palm Springs Life feature 'Chef Nyitre Rodgers Elevates the Menu at The Edge Steakhouse' confirms; OpenTable listing also references her).
Featured in Palm Springs Life (chef feature article exists, confirming editorial coverage).
Stable Marriott/Ritz-Carlton resort ownership (Marriott Bonvoy dining directory listing confirms current brand affiliation).
contradicts: Domain attribution claim: brief states 'theedgesteakhouse.com belongs to the Westgate Las Vegas brand.' Independent verification shows the OPPOSITE — Westgate Las Vegas uses westgateresorts.com for its EDGE Steakhouse, while theedgesteakhouse.com hosts content for the Rancho Mirage Ritz-Carlton location ('Steakhouses in Palm Springs California', 'Best Steakhouse in California', 'meet-the-chef' page references Nyitre Rodgers). The Rancho Mirage venue DOES appear to operate the independent domain — reducing (not increasing) entity-disambiguation risk for agents. This flips the assessment's discovery-surface conclusion.
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable ~1500+ review count (already self-flagged as UNVERIFIED in brief; could not fetch OpenTable directly).

Exact title of Nyitre Rodgers — sources conflict: some call her 'executive chef,' another ('5reasonstovisit.com') describes her as 'chef de cuisine' who took the role in May 2025. Brief uses 'executive chef'; this may be imprecise. Original 2015 opening team had Bruno Lopez as Executive Chef.
Locale Magazine date-night/steakhouse roundup features (not surfaced in independent search).

James Beard / Michelin / Food Network credentials — none claimed in the brief and none found in independent search (consistent with brief positioning the venue as losing pure-credential queries to Agua Caliente).
Claim about JSON-LD/schema.org markup absence on Ritz-Carlton page — not independently inspected.
Claim that venue 'often appears second to The Steakhouse at Agua Caliente in award-led queries' — query-surface behavior not independently tested.
input.notes: Core facts (opening date, Tripadvisor #8/80/187 reviews/4.4, Yelp 315 reviews, current chef Nyitre Rodgers, Ritz-Carlton/Marriott ownership) all hold up. One material contradiction: the brief inverts the theedgesteakhouse.com domain attribution — that domain serves the Rancho Mirage venue, not Westgate Las Vegas (Westgate uses westgateresorts.com). This is a load-bearing error because it changes the discovery-surface and entity-disambiguation narrative. Chef title appears to be 'chef de cuisine' per Palm Springs Life/5reasonstovisit sources (since May 2025), not 'executive chef' — worth correcting. No James Beard or Michelin credentials surfaced (none claimed), consistent with the credential-light positioning.
